top10.introHtml: Ranked by top condition, model reliability, and value — the best cheap fun convertibles within 35 miles of Media, PA as of May 28, 2026.

picks.whyItRocks: The 2.8L inline-6 Z3 is the sweet spot of the model range — balanced power and chassis, short wheelbase, rear-wheel drive. 75k miles is mid-life for a BMW six, and $7,940 from a private seller gives real negotiating room. The Z3 2.8 is increasingly collectible, meaning values are stable. ✓ Verify: Operate the soft top through a full cycle; inspect corner seams and rear window seam for tears. Check rocker panels and rear floor for rust. Oil leaks at valve cover and VANOS seals are routine — ask what's been addressed. Verify power window regulators work. Bottom line: Best dollar-per-smile in the entire batch — a 2.8 Z3 for under $8k is hard to find.

E88 128i convertible is a rewarding driver's car — naturally-aspirated N52 engine, no torque steer, rear-wheel drive. 90k miles is well within the engine's lifespan. 'Good Deal' badge on CarGurus. At $7,995 it's priced like a base commuter but drives like a sports car. ✓ Verify: Power top: operate through full cycle, check headliner for water stains (top seams at A-pillar common leak point). N52 thermostat housing (plastic — replace preventatively if not done). Listen for oil consumption on cold start. Check folding mechanism operation. Bottom line: Daily-driveable BMW fun for under $8k with a strong reliability record on the N52 engine.

GXP = turbocharged 2.0L Ecotec making 260hp — this transforms the Solstice from cute roadster to genuinely quick. 51k miles is low for a 2007. The GXP is the enthusiast-sought trim; base Solstice owners often wish they had held out for it. ✓ Verify: Turbocharger: check boost pressure and listen for wastegate rattle. Soft top: operate fully, inspect rear window for yellowing and seam integrity. Rocker panels and top-mount-to-rocker brackets are the critical Solstice rust inspection points. Look for oil consumption. Bottom line: Turbocharged roadster at 51k miles — rare combo of low mileage and the right engine.

'Great Deal' rating on CarGurus, 69k miles, and the E88 platform is well-sorted by the 2013 model year. The 128i's N52 engine is legendarily bulletproof compared to the turbocharged N55 in the 135i. Fairless Hills is 35 miles out but the deal rating justifies the drive. ✓ Verify: Full soft-top cycle, headliner water stains, A-pillar seal condition. N52 valve cover gasket and VANOS seals — common at this mileage. Verify convertible top pump operation and check for hydraulic fluid leaks under the trunk liner. Bottom line: Great Deal badge means the market agrees — strong pick if you can get there to inspect.

At $7,500 with 63k miles in Telford (27mi), this is the cheapest path to a fun two-seat roadster in the area. CarGurus rates it a 'Great Deal.' The Solstice base 2.4L Ecotec is reliable; the fun-to-price ratio is unmatched at this figure. ✓ Verify: Soft top: full operation cycle, rear window clarity, seam integrity. Rockers and top-mount brackets for rust (Solstice-specific weak spot). Verify no timing chain noise (2.4L Ecotec chain tensioner can wear). Bottom line: Cheapest fun convertible in the search — go inspect it before someone else does.

48k miles is genuinely low for 2013 — makes this the best-mileage MINI in the search. The Roadster S trim adds the turbocharged 1.6L engine and sport suspension. Two-seat layout sharpens the handling vs. the 4-seat Cooper convertible. MINI reliability is reasonable below 100k. ✓ Verify: Top operation and rear window seal (MINI Roadster rear-window seam can leak). Turbo boost under load. Timing chain tensioner — preventative replacement due at ~60-80k on Prince-engine cars. Check for coolant leaks at thermostat housing. Bottom line: 48k miles on a sporty 2-seat roadster is the standout value in the MINI camp.

The SLK 230's retractable hardtop is the big advantage here — no soft-top wear, weathertight year-round, and the folding mechanism is elegantly reliable. At 68k miles and $8,495, the supercharged 2.3L M111 engine is proven. West Chester dealer is local. ✓ Verify: Retractable hardtop: operate through 3 full cycles, listen for grinding or hesitation (hydraulic actuators can seize). Check for hydraulic fluid leaks. ASR/traction control function. Verify the soft-top well drain tubes are clear (water intrusion if blocked). Bottom line: Best year-round pick — no soft-top worries and local to Media.

C4 Corvette convertible with only 50k miles for $8,450 is exceptional V8 value. The 1994 LT1 5.7L V8 makes 300hp and sounds magnificent. Private seller means no dealer markup. The C4 convertible is increasingly appreciated as the most affordable entry into V8 Corvette ownership. ✓ Verify: Soft top: full operation, rear window condition (C4 plastic windows yellow and crack). LT1 optispark distributor — critical inspection (under water pump, known failure point). Check for oil leaks at rear main seal and valve covers. Frame rails for rust. Bottom line: Most V8 horsepower per dollar in the search — and a genuine American sports car.

Mid-engine layout gives the MR2 a 50/50 weight balance no front-engine car can match. At 74k miles this Toyota powerplant has decades left. The targa-top design keeps the experience open-air. Rare to find in budget — these are being snapped up as future classics. ✓ Verify: Targa panels: check seals for water intrusion, store easily in the trunk. 1ZZ engine: check for oil burning above 75k (add oil between changes is common). Rear sill rust is MR2-specific — inspect carefully. Listen for exhaust manifold cracks on cold start. Bottom line: The most exotic driving experience in the budget — mid-engine balance you can't replicate at this price.

30,483 miles on a 2007 is the lowest-mileage roadster in the entire search. The Saturn Sky shares its Kappa platform with the Pontiac Solstice — same fun formula, potentially garage-kept based on mileage. Douglassville is 28 miles out. ✓ Verify: Verify mileage against CarFax — this is suspiciously low and worth confirming. Soft top seals (can dry-rot from sitting even with low miles). Rocker rust inspection. Check that the 2.4L engine hasn't developed timing chain noise from sitting. Bottom line: If the CarFax confirms 30k miles, this is the standout value — a practically new roadster at a fraction of original cost.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles in budget — ranked GT first, then modern V6. All within 35 miles of Media, PA.

picks.whyItRocks: 1994 Mustang GT convertible with only 44,000 miles — last year of the beloved 5.0L pushrod V8 before the modular engine swap. Clean title, no accidents per Cars.com filters. Eddington PA is 29 miles away. 44k miles on a 31-year-old car means it was driven sparingly and likely well cared-for. ✓ Verify: SN-95 convertible-specific: inspect the top-mount-to-rocker panel seam for rust (common failure point where the convertible top bracket meets the unibody). T-5 manual or AOD-E automatic — shift through all gears slowly. Rear 8.8 axle: check for clunk on takeoff. Top operation full cycle. Bottom line: Last of the 5.0L Mustang GT convertibles at 44k miles — a legit piece of American muscle history.

1995 GT convertible is the final year of the SN-95 body paired with the 5.0L V8 (1996 switched to the 4.6L SOHC). 55k miles is solid. GT Motors is a Mustang-specialist dealer, which gives some confidence in the prep. ✓ Verify: Same SN-95 convertible checklist: rocker/top-mount rust, 8.8 rear axle clunk, T-5 third-gear synchro (slow-shift to 3rd while cold). Check for frame rust at convertible-top mounting points. Verify 5.0L firing on all 8 cylinders. Bottom line: The last 5.0L-powered Mustang GT convertible — a satisfying V8 drop-top for the money.

Most modern Mustang convertible in the search — S197 body, 305hp 3.7L V6, proper six-speed manual available. 'Good Deal' badge on CarGurus. 61k miles with clean-title filtering. Sewell NJ is only 20 miles away. ✓ Verify: S197 convertible: check rear seats and carpet for water staining from top seal failure (common leak at rear corners). Inspect IRS subframe bushings if equipped. T6060 six-speed: test slow-shift to 3rd. Rear axle clunk on takeoff. Bottom line: The practical modern pick — better safety, more power than you'd expect from a V6, and locally available.
